Q: How is the name of the library related to the statue in the park in front of it?
A: The name of the library, as seen on the building, is "НАРОДНА БИБЛИОТЕКА КИРИЛ И МЕТОДИЙ" (National Library "Cyril and Methodius"). The statue in the park in front of the library depicts Saints Cyril and Methodius, who are historically significant for creating the Glagolitic alphabet, the precursor to the Cyrillic script used in Bulgarian and other Slavic languages. Thus, the library is named after the two brothers, and the statue honors them, directly relating the name of the library to the figures represented by the statue.
C: The statue is a monument, honoring the brothers Cyril and Methodius, after whom the library is named.

Q: Why is the library named after Cyril and Methodius?
A: The library is named after Cyril and Methodius because they are celebrated as the creators of the Glagolitic alphabet, the first alphabet used for the Slavonic manuscripts, and are highly regarded for their contribution to Slavic literacy and culture. Their work laid the foundation for the Cyrillic script, which is used in many Slavic languages today. The library honors their legacy in education, literature, and cultural development.
C: The brothers Cyril and Methodius invented the first Bulgarian alphabet which is why the library is named after them.
